articles.description:
Entering the new year, Crumbl has switched up its weekly rotating dessert menu so that there is now a permanent Classics Menu with six flavors and four rotating "specialties" each week.

From January 5 through January 10, 2026, the limited-time desserts at Crumbl include:
- New Dubai Biscoff Brownie - A chocolate brownie topped with a kataifi and Biscoff cookie butter filling, a white drop layer, and a drizzle of Biscoff cookie butter.
- New Chocolate Vanilla Ice Cream Swirl Cookie - A brown sugar cookie packed with waffle cone bites, topped with a chocolate-vanilla mousse swirl and more waffle cone bites.
- New Oatmeal Marshmallow Cookie - A spiced oatmeal cookie topped with marshmallow cream cheese frosting.
- Ultimate Peanut Butter Cookie - A warm peanut butter cookie stuffed with peanut butter and topped with a melted peanut butter drizzle.
The permanent Classics Menu includes the following cookies:
- Semi-sweet Chocolate Chunk Cookie - A warm cookie filled with semi-sweet chocolate chunks and a sprinkle of flaky sea salt.
- Pink Sugar Cookie - An almond sugar cookie topped with a pink almond frosting.
- Chocolate Crumb Cookie featuring Oreo - A warm chocolate cookie made with Oreo cookie pieces, topped with vanilla cream cheese frosting and more Oreo cookie pieces. 
- Brownie Batter Cookie - A warm chocolate cookie packed with semi-sweet chocolate chips and topped with brownie batter.
- Snickerdoodle Cookie - A warm vanilla sugar cookie rolled in a cinnamon-sugar coating.
- Celebration Cake Cookie - A warm cake batter cookie topped with cake batter cream cheese frosting and rainbow sprinkles.
Photo via Crumbl.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Crumbl's Weekly Rotating Menu Through January 10, 2026

articles.description:
As I mentioned earlier on the BrandEating Patreon, I received a Ninja Swirl Creami ice cream maker to experiment with courtesy of SharkNinja. I'm going to use it to see how and if various fast-food desserts could be made into ice cream or soft serve. I decided to start off with something easy: Turning Wendy's Chocolate Frosty into soft serve. It's almost soft serve already, so I figured, why not? I'm calling the segment, "Will it Creami?" ("Will it Swirl Creami" just doesn't sound right)

The Process
 I purchased a large Wendy's Frosty for $3.59 and let it melt slightly to reduce its volume. I then poured it into the Creami's supplied pint container and placed it into the freezer for a little over a day. After it was good and frozen, I ran it through the processing section of the Cremi (it took four minutes), which finely shaves and blends the frozen Frosty into a soft serve consistency (it's like the reverse of your typical ice cream maker, where you churn and freeze the ice cream at the same time).
Once it was churned, I attached the container to the soft serve dispensing section of the Creami. There, it was forced/pressed the Frosty soft serve through a nozzle to achieve that signature swirled look. If you don't want/need to have that swirled soft serve appearance, you can skip this step and just spoon the soft serve out of the container.



How it turned out
The Wendy's Frosty Soft Serve was pretty good. Obviously, it had the same mild chocolate and hint of vanilla flavor of a regular Frosty, but it also had the firmer, smoother, less melty texture of soft serve (my dispensing skills need improvement, however). It was a little too firm, though, and I wonder if that was due to the relatively low fat content of a Frosty (fat/cream makes ice cream softer) or if I should have given the soft serve another blend (there's a "re-spin" function on the machine). Still, the firmness wasn't outside the range of other soft serves that I've eaten.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Will It Creami? Wendy's Chocolate Frosty

Jersey Mike's introduces a limited-time meal deal called the Boardwalk Bundle.

Priced at $10.99, the Boardwalk Bundle includes a regular #2 sub (AKA the Jersey Shore's Favorite), a small bag of chips, and a 22-oz fountain beverage.
The #2 sub consists of provolone, ham, and cappacuolo, plus your choice of veggies, condiments, and bread. Prices vary, but it normally costs $9.95 here in Los Angeles (which amounts to a bag of chips and a drink for $1).
The Boardwalk Bundle is available now at participating Jersey Mike's locations.
Photo via Jersey Mike's.

articles.description:
Taco Bell is testing "Everyday Nacho Fries" for a limited time at participating locations in the Charlotte, NC area.

Everyday Nacho Fries include Supreme Nacho Fries and Loaded Taco Nacho Fries. The test locations appear to be serving one or the other, but not both. Each is priced at $4.99.
Supreme Nacho Fries feature seasoned fries topped with seasoned beef, Spicy Ranch sauce, warm nacho cheese sauce, reduced-fat sour cream, diced tomatoes, and a 3-cheese blend (mozzarella, pepper jack, and cheddar).
Loaded Taco Nacho Fries feature seasoned fries topped with seasoned beef, warm nacho cheese sauce, shredded lettuce, diced tomatoes, shredded cheddar cheese, Spicy Ranch sauce, and crispy Fiesta Strips.
Loaded Taco Nacho Fries were previously released nationally as a limited-time item, while Supreme Nacho Fries are very similar to the limited-time Nacho Fries Supreme that accompanied the initial launch of Taco Bell's Nacho Fries. Given the "everyday" moniker, it looks like Taco Bell is exploring adding a topped Nacho Fries dish to its menu permanently.
Photo via Taco Bell.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Taco Bell Tests Supreme and Loaded Taco Nacho Fries

articles.description:
Cake bakery chain Nothing Bundt Cakes reveals its upcoming limited-time selection of bundt cakes in January and February 2026. The upcoming lineup is as follows:

- Biscoff Cookie Butter Cake - A returning flavor that sold out at 233 locations during its 2025 debut, the Biscoff Cookie Butter Cake features a spice cake with Biscoff Cookie Butter baked into the batter and Biscoff cookies baked into its base and layered on top. Biscoff Cookie Butter is also drizzled on top. The flavor is available from January 5 through February 15, 2026, while supplies last in all cake sizes
- New-Fashioned Marble With Chocolate Frosting -  A yellow-and-chocolate marble cake topped with chocolate ganache cream cheese frosting (this is the first time that Nothing Bundt Cakes has offered a chocolate frosting) and chocolate-coated wafers. It's available from January 12 through January 25, 2026, in Bundtlet size only, while supplies last.
- New Chocolate Raspberry Heart Made with Dove (pictured) - Ready for Valentine's Day, this chocolate cake is filled with raspberry puree and topped with signature cream cheese frosting, a drizzle of raspberry puree, and a Dove chocolate heart. It's available from February 2 through February 15, 2026, in Bundtlet size only, while supplies last.
Photo via Nothing Bundt Cakes.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Nothing Bundt Cake Reveals Limited-Time Cakes for January and February 2026

articles.description:
Arby's introduces its take on an Italian beef sandwich with the launch of the new Italian Beef Dip. The chain also adds a new Peach Cobbler Roll.

Available for a limited time, the new sandwich features thin-sliced roast beef, Swiss cheese, and giardiniera, served on a warm sub roll with au jus for dipping. It's basically Arby's French Dip, only with the addition of giardiniera.
Prices vary, but the Italian Beef Dip costs $7.19 (versus $7.09 for a French Dip) at an Arby's restaurant here in Los Angeles.
The new Dip differs slightly from the test item from last year. It now includes cheese and lacks the premium top round roast beef that was being tested.



The Peach Cobbler Roll is a new, limited-time dessert that comes two to an order. It features a rolled-up crispy pastry stuffed with peaches, graham cracker, and bourbon vanilla filling. An order costs $2.89 near me.
Photos via Arby's.
